foo-fighters-1944.name: Foo Fighters (1944)
foo-fighters-1944.summary: Wartime Allied pilots report glowing balls pacing aircraft; later attributed to ball lightning, St Elmo's fire, and reflections.

arnold-1947.name: Kenneth Arnold sighting (1947)
arnold-1947.summary: Civilian pilot reports nine objects flying in formation 'like saucers skipping on water' — origin of 'flying saucer'.

roswell-1947.name: Roswell Incident (1947)
roswell-1947.summary: Recovery of debris near Roswell Army Air Field initially announced as a 'flying disc' before retraction.

mantell-1948.name: Mantell incident (1948)
mantell-1948.summary: P-51 pilot Thomas Mantell killed pursuing a UAP later identified as a Skyhook balloon.

mariana-film-1950.name: Mariana film (1950)
mariana-film-1950.summary: 16mm film by Nicholas Mariana of two silvery objects over a baseball stadium; investigated by USAF, never resolved.

washington-dc-1952.name: Washington D.C. flap (1952)
washington-dc-1952.summary: Two consecutive weekends of radar/visual UAP encounters over D.C. that prompted CIA's Robertson Panel.

soccoro-1964.name: Lonnie Zamora landing (1964)
soccoro-1964.summary: Police officer reports egg-shaped craft with two occupants; physical traces at landing site documented by USAF investigators.

malmstrom-1967.name: Malmstrom missile shutdown (1967)
malmstrom-1967.summary: Multiple Minuteman ICBMs go offline coincident with reported UAP overflight; deputy commander Robert Salas testimony.

iran-1976.name: Imperial Iranian Air Force F-4 chase (1976)
iran-1976.summary: Two F-4 Phantoms vector on a brilliant UAP that disabled their weapons and instruments; DIA cable widely cited.

rendlesham-1980.name: Rendlesham Forest (1980)
rendlesham-1980.summary: U.S. Air Force personnel reported lights and an object near RAF Bentwaters/Woodbridge.

jal-1628-1986.name: JAL 1628 (1986)
jal-1628-1986.summary: Japan Air Lines 747 cargo flight tracks a giant UAP over Alaska; FAA radar corroboration.

belgian-wave-1989.name: Belgian wave (1989-1990)
belgian-wave-1989.summary: Months of triangular UFO sightings over Belgium with corroborating F-16 radar lock and military investigation.

phoenix-lights-1997.name: Phoenix Lights (1997)
phoenix-lights-1997.summary: Mass-witness V-formation of lights observed over Arizona; one of the most-reported civilian UAP events.

ohare-2006.name: O'Hare disc (2006)
ohare-2006.summary: United Airlines ground crew and pilots report a metallic disc hovering over Gate C17, departing through cloud layer leaving a hole.

stephenville-2008.name: Stephenville (2008)
stephenville-2008.summary: Multi-witness UAP sighting in central Texas with corroborating radar tracks reported by MUFON.

nimitz-tic-tac-2004.name: Nimitz Tic Tac (2004)
nimitz-tic-tac-2004.summary: USS Nimitz F/A-18s under Cdr Fravor encounter a Tic-Tac shaped object descending from 80,000 ft; ATFLIR footage later released.

aguadilla-2013.name: Aguadilla (CBP, 2013)
aguadilla-2013.summary: Customs and Border Protection thermal video of a low-altitude object that appeared to enter the ocean and split.

eastern-seaboard-2014-2019.name: Eastern Seaboard sightings (2014–2019)
eastern-seaboard-2014-2019.summary: Sustained observations of UAP by Navy aviators training along the East Coast through the 2010s.

gimbal-2015.name: Gimbal (USS Theodore Roosevelt, 2015)
gimbal-2015.location: Off the coast of Jacksonville, FL
gimbal-2015.summary: FLIR ATFLIR pod video from a Super Hornet showing an apparently rotating, hovering object against the wind.

go-fast-2015.name: Go Fast (USS Theodore Roosevelt, 2015)
go-fast-2015.location: Off the East Coast of the U.S.
go-fast-2015.summary: FLIR video of a small object speeding low over the ocean as tracked by an F/A-18.

aaro.name: All-domain Anomaly Resolution Office
aaro.summary: DoD office responsible for UAP investigation since 2022; publisher of historical record reviews.
